spec.summary: NPC–NPC encounters: how they take turns autonomously.
spec.detail: When autonomous actors advance, use the meter queue already in runtime state instead of a one-off static sort. Each eligible actor gains initiative from agility plus entity-kind weighting, becomes ready at threshold `100`, and spends one threshold chunk when acting. Multiple actors may act inside one player turn if they are ready, but the deterministic cap per player turn stays bounded. Queue order, readiness, and meter values should remain visible through runtime status/debug so perf work and UX work share the same source of truth.

Current direction: keep initiative meter state in the engine (`initiativeMeters`, `lastInitiativeOrder`, surfaced `initiativeQueue` status rows), drive gains primarily from agility, and use entity-kind weighting so bosses feel urgent without random hidden boosts. Extend this queue before inventing a separate async scheduling model: workers/background computation should execute deterministic queue steps, not bypass the queue logic.

Use the package-local perf harness as the working budget gate for now: average turn resolution <= `700ms`, p95 <= `1200ms`, max <= `1600ms` across the deterministic move-run sample. Navigation render timing should continue logging structured debug events when a frame crosses the 16ms threshold so we can separate board redraw cost from overlay/input cost.

spec.summary: Board presence and portrait sprite rules.
spec.detail: Small-scale board markers should prioritize fast recognition over fidelity: boss crown/portrait, hostile marker, dungeoneer marker, then room icon. The bottom room panel can use a portrait-scale bust or emblem, but it should resolve from the same room-presence summary and fallback order as the board. If authored room media arrives later, it layers onto this priority system rather than replacing it with bespoke per-screen rules.

spec.summary: Utility spells: conversation, transportation, exploration, detection.
spec.detail: Utility spells span spell categories. Conversation: unlock a specific dialogue option only (no roll bonus); duration = 1 encounter; e.g. persuasion, intimidation, charm, truth, fear, empathy, bargain. Transportation: movement speed (haste), teleport (short hop, floor recall). Exploration/detection: light, detect traps, scout ahead. Cost = cast (mana). All have categoryId and rarityId; filter by spell-categories pack.

What Fame does (rewards, unlocks, narrative); how livestream grants Fame; deed propagation (information spread) and effect on world/NPCs; content schema for fame rewards and deed definitions. Livestream and dungeon events should share one live feed with actor-aware MMO-style prefixes so the player can skim system/player/boss/dungeoneer/live updates quickly.
Recruitment and dismissal; behavior in combat and in rooms; interaction with traits/dialogue; UI (companion panel); persistence; how Fame or other systems gate companion options.

Confirm one slot-assignment model across Spellbook and rune forge: prepared slots on the left, pool/grid in the center, detail portrait on the right, and codex only when rune-forge context enables it. Encounter view still uses slotted spells only. Slot count is fixed in content/config (not level-gated). Reusable components: slot list, pool picker, assign flow, shared detail pane.

spec.summary: Entity type, archetype, occupation, party role, titles.
spec.detail: Four layers: entity type (what can spawn; physical); archetype (build: stat mods + special rules; from title); occupation (dungeoneer, merchant, boss); party role (for dungeoneers: Jack of all trades, tank, healer, …). Kael = dungeoneer, party role Jack of all trades, solos. Title = unlock (predicate list) + archetypeId + rarity; not all titles same. Quests: main vs side. Packs: entityTypePack, archetypePack, titlePack, occupationPack, partyRolePack. ~50 titles, ~50 archetypes; codegen-ready.

How narrator and NPC dialogue are displayed (layout, speaker label, typewriter vs instant); room-entry narration. Substantial content: narrator lines, room flavour, general info; dialogue trees and narration pack in content.

Which rooms have Search (content: room feature or chest object); loot tables per chest/room; one-shot or cooldown; schema for treasureChestRef/lootTableId. Combat rewards = mana crystals (and optionally more) on win.
Trait schema (content-defined dimensions); how dialogue/narration gates on traits; NPC AI reading traits; alignment with existing engine trait/feature vectors.
XP sources (combat, objectives, deeds); XP thresholds and level curve; what level grants (stats, slot count, unlocks); content-driven tables.

Gameplay UI (movement, room actions, interaction view, HUD) vs display UI (Map, Bag, Journal, Spellbook, Stats, Equipped, Settings). Primitives → components → composite views → screens/flows. Nomenclature and spec/build order (primitives first, then gameplay components, LocationView/HUD, InteractionView, display screens, MainMenu/GameScreen). Component registry or storybook.

Full inventory: rarities, entityTypes, archetypes, occupations, partyRoles, titles, predicates, spells, evolutions, runes, rooms, actions, encounters, bosses, items, effects, quests. All reference shared ids (rarityId, archetypeId, etc.). Rebalancing = tuning so new content doesn't break difficulty/economy/fairness. Tooling: validators (schema + refs), balance calculators (curves, economy, power band), diff on new content.

gap.summary: How we navigate the dungeon overall and what that view is; what out-of-dungeon looks like.

detail.item: In-dungeon: what the player sees (layout, fog, discovery), how they pick exits, how discovery/fog state is stored and displayed. Tied to dark maps and map content.
Out-of-dungeon: what it looks like when the player gets out (overworld, hub, town, or other).

gap.approach: See build-approach above: no tile-based movement; unified graph; one location-view; room/hub visuals composed from dungeon tileset (static or light animation); PokeAPI for entities; fog = visited set. Hub map must be buildable and referenceable in content. Remaining: overworld/hub schema (places + map layout), and tile-composition format per room/place.

gap.summary: Define how "dark maps" are represented and in which schema.

resolved.item: Maps are items (or item kind "map") in content-source / item pack. Map-specific field: completeness (0–1); isDarkMap = completeness below threshold (e.g. 0.9).
"Negative" = when sold: lower price and/or reputation penalty. Schema: itemKind "map", completenessThreshold; balance numbers for sell price and reputation delta.

gap.depends: Discovery/fog and where it lives is part of gap dungeon-navigation-view.

gap.summary: Effect/type/status system; align with PokeAPI types.

resolved.item: Full Pokemon-style status set. Types in schema (PokeAPI-aligned); skills/spells reference type for display and formula.
Status conditions as content; entities have current status list; UI highlights type/status. Effect application: target kind entity | object | self.
Effect formulas live in content (not action-formulas only—content owns the tunable formulas).

gap.summary: When do we show the interaction (encounter-style) view; one scene, mode-driven.

resolved.item: Only when the room has something to do: entity, object (e.g. campfire), or event requiring focus. Empty rooms / no events = no interaction view; stay in dungeon navigation.
One scene, mode-driven (combat | dialogue | rune_forge | cutscene). Same layout: target slot + action panels; mode drives content. Navigation available where appropriate.
Concept: "interaction view" or "room focus" rather than "encounter" so it's clear not every room is an encounter.

gap.summary: Balance numbers in content; designers must be able to tune correctly.

resolved.item: Spell/skill: power, accuracy, type, cost, status application chance. Items: price, sell value, effect strength, map completeness threshold.
Combat: flee formula coefficients, spawn weights. Economy: map sell formula, dark-map penalty. Rune forge: crafting cost, breed requirements. Status: duration, tick, stacking.
All in content-source or content-pack config; Unreal consumes same contracts. Ensure schema and docs (and tooling if needed) let designers tune without code changes.

gap.summary: Spell crafting system: full design spec.
gap.detail: See spell-crafting-spec above. Order of runes in composition: matters. Evolution naming and affinity decay resolved in checklist-resolutions (content-driven names, no player rename; no affinity decay). Remaining: effect assignment and type/status link when we lock effect-types content.
